yield.t:

vt. 出产, 给予, 让出, 放弃, 使屈服
vi. 出产, 屈服, 投降, 倒塌
yield.d:
n. an amount of a product
v. be the cause or source of
v. give in, as to influence or pressure
v. bring in

束手就擒, 投降受俘
[法] 投降当俘虏

yield strength.t:
[计] 屈变力, 屈服强度
[化] 屈服强度

yield temperature.t: [化] 屈服温度; 流动温度

yield the palm to sb.t: 承认被某人打败, 输给某人

yield the palm to sb..t: 承认输给, 承认不如...好

yield to maturity.t: [经] 到期日收益(报酬), 到期报酬率

yield to necessity.t: 为生活所迫,迫不得已,出于无奈

yieldance.d:
n. The act of producing; yield; as, the yieldance of the
   earth.
n. The act of yielding; concession.

yielding.t:
a. 生产的, 屈从的, 柔顺的, 易弯曲的
[化] 屈服
yielding.d:
s. inclined to yield to argument or influence or control
s. lacking stiffness and giving way to pressure
s. tending to give in or surrender or agree

yielding point.t:
[计] 屈服点, 拐点
[化] 屈服点

yieldingly.t: adv. 易弯曲地, 柔顺地

yieldly.t: adv. 屈服地;生产地;支付地

yields.t: n. 生产量, 投资效益（yield的复数形式）
yields.d:
n production of a certain amount
n the income or profit arising from such transactions as the sale of land or other property
n an amount of a product
n the quantity of something (as a commodity) that is created (usually within a given period of time)
v be the cause or source of
v end resistance, as under pressure or force
v give or supply
v give over; surrender or relinquish to the physical control of another
v give in, as to influence or pressure
v move in order to make room for someone for something
v cause to happen or be responsible for
v be willing to concede
v be fatally overwhelmed
v bring in
v be flexible under stress of physical force
v cease opposition; stop fighting
v consent relu
